findings.title: Notification Priority Levels Implementation
findings.description: Implementing a three-tier priority system for notifications requires changes to the notification rendering logic and user interface components. This will also necessitate updates to the backend to support priority levels.
assumptions: The existing notification system can be modified to support priority levels.

evidence.excerpt: Propose a three-tier priority system that visually differentiates urgency.

recommendations: Design UI mockups for priority levels.
Update backend to handle priority levels.

findings.title: Push Notification Delivery Failure on Android 14
findings.description: The failure of push notifications on Android 14 devices poses a critical risk to user engagement. This issue needs immediate resolution to prevent loss of users.
assumptions: The issue is solely related to Android 14 and can be fixed with a software update.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate and resolve the push notification issue on Android 14.

findings.title: Implement ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: Implementing ML-based notification batching will require a robust ML model capable of learning user preferences. This will involve data collection, model training, and integration into the notification system.
assumptions: The ML model can be trained with existing user engagement data.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Define the data requirements for the ML model.
Plan for model training and integration.

findings.title: User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Creating a user preference page requires a new API endpoint and a React component. This will also need to respect existing global mute settings.
assumptions: The existing API can be extended to support new endpoints.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Develop the API endpoint for user preferences.
Create the React component for the settings page.

findings.title: Reduce Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: To achieve the latency goal of under 200ms, the delivery pipeline must be re-architected to use asynchronous writes and batch inserts. This change is complex and requires thorough testing.
assumptions: The current architecture can support asynchronous processing.

evidence.excerpt: Root cause appears to be synchronous database writes in the delivery pipeline.

recommendations: Plan for architectural changes to the delivery pipeline.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with the SmartNotify project. Key components such as notification priority levels and delivery latency require immediate attention, while ML-based batching and user preferences can be phased into later versions. The findings highlight the need for architectural changes and the resolution of platform-specific issues to ensure successful delivery.
